description:
Phase categories (ui_ux, research, write, review, testing, requirements) are hardcoded as string match arms in 3 separate files with inconsistent member sets:
- `protocol/src/model_routing.rs` (is_ui_ux_phase, is_research_phase, is_review_phase, is_requirements_phase, is_testing_phase)
- `daemon_scheduler_phase_targets.rs` (is_ui_ux_phase, is_research_phase — different UI/UX set)
- `phase_executor.rs` (write-phase set hardcoded 3 times in same file)

Solution: Define phase capabilities as data in the workflow/agent-runtime config. Each phase definition should declare capabilities like `can_write`, `needs_commit`, `model_group`, `parse_research_signal`, `safety_rules_template`. The daemon reads these from config instead of branching on string matches. This is the single highest-impact DRY fix — it touches model routing, phase execution, and phase target planning.
title: Extract phase category membership into config-driven phase capabilities
